title: The Job Interview
description: Test your understanding of the video and practise useful job interview vocabulary, office words and common expressions.

questions.question: What is an “iron”?
options: A device used to remove creases from clothes
A document with your work experience
A spinning door at an office entrance
questions.answer: A device used to remove creases from clothes
questions.explanation: An iron is the device Sune uses to remove creases from his shirt.

questions.question: What is an “ironing board”?
options: A narrow padded table used while ironing clothes
A desk where an interviewer sits
A board that shows job offers
A mirror used before an interview
questions.answer: A narrow padded table used while ironing clothes
questions.explanation: An ironing board is the narrow padded table where you rest clothes while ironing them.

questions.question: What is a “suit”?
options: A matching jacket and trousers worn for formal occasions
A long piece of fabric worn around the collar
A cord worn around your neck with an ID badge
A document that summarises your skills
questions.answer: A matching jacket and trousers worn for formal occasions
questions.explanation: Sune wears a suit for his job interview.

questions.question: What is a “tie”?
options: A long piece of fabric worn around the collar of a shirt
questions.answer: A long piece of fabric worn around the collar of a shirt
questions.explanation: Sune wears a tie with his shirt and suit.

questions.question: What is a “revolving door”?
options: A circular spinning door at the entrance of large buildings
A door that is always locked
A small door inside an interview room
A door used only by receptionists
questions.answer: A circular spinning door at the entrance of large buildings
questions.explanation: Sune walks through a revolving door when he arrives at the office building.

questions.question: What is a “lanyard”?
options: A cord worn around your neck with an ID badge attached
A document with your education and work experience
A chair in a waiting room
questions.answer: A cord worn around your neck with an ID badge attached
questions.explanation: The receptionist gives Sune a lanyard with an ID badge.

questions.question: What is a “CV”?
options: A document that summarises your work experience, education and skills
A small badge worn around your neck
A question asked in an interview
questions.answer: A document that summarises your work experience, education and skills
questions.explanation: Sune reads his CV while waiting for the interview.

questions.question: What does “go blank” mean?
options: Your mind suddenly becomes empty and you cannot think of what to say
questions.answer: Your mind suddenly becomes empty and you cannot think of what to say
questions.explanation: Sune's mind goes blank when he gets an unexpected question.

questions.question: What does “give it your best shot” mean?
options: To try as hard as you possibly can
questions.answer: To try as hard as you possibly can
questions.explanation: Sune gives the interview his best shot, even though he is not sure how it went.

sections.title: Video comprehension
sections.instructions: Answer the questions about the story.

questions.question: What does Sune have today?
questions.explanation: At the beginning of the video, Sune has a job interview today.

questions.question: What does Sune do before leaving?
questions.explanation: Sune spends twenty minutes ironing his shirt before the interview.

questions.question: Who gives Sune a lanyard?
questions.explanation: At the reception desk, the receptionist asks for his name and hands him a lanyard.

questions.question: What does Sune read in the waiting room?
questions.explanation: Sune sits in the waiting room with his CV on his lap and reads it one more time.

questions.question: Which question makes Sune's mind go blank?
options: Where do you see yourself in five years?
What would you like to eat?
questions.answer: Where do you see yourself in five years?
questions.explanation: Sune is not expecting this question, and his mind goes blank.

sections.title: Complete the sentence
sections.instructions: Choose the correct word or expression.

questions.question: Before the interview, Sune uses an ______ to remove creases from his shirt.
questions.explanation: An iron is used to remove creases from clothes.

questions.question: At reception, Sune receives a ______ with an ID badge attached.
questions.explanation: A lanyard is a cord worn around your neck with an ID badge attached.

questions.question: When the interviewer asked a difficult question, Sune's mind went ______.
questions.explanation: If your mind goes blank, you suddenly cannot think of what to say.
